rules.name: readme-before-subdir-edit
rules.evidence: Before editing or creating files in any subdirectory (e.g., packages/*, crates/*), read all README.md files in the directory path from the repo root up to and including the target file's directory.

rules.name: readme-before-subdir-create
rules.evidence: Before editing or creating files in any subdirectory (e.g., packages/*, crates/*), read all README.md files in the directory path from the repo root up to and including the target file's directory.

rules.name: start-dev-watch-before-source-edit
rules.evidence: If you are changing Next.js source or integration tests, start `pnpm --filter=next dev` in a separate terminal session before making edits (unless it is already running). If you skip this, explicitly state why.

rules.name: rebuild-after-branch-checkout
rules.evidence: Always run a full bootstrap build after switching branches: git checkout <branch> ; pnpm build-all

rules.name: rebuild-before-integration-tests
rules.evidence: When running Next.js integration tests, you must rebuild if source files have changed

rules.name: match-ci-env-vars-when-testing
rules.evidence: Always match the exact CI env vars the CI job uses ... Key differences such as IS_WEBPACK_TEST=1 can change bundler selection and snapshot output

rules.name: new-test-generation-mandatory
rules.evidence: Generating tests using `pnpm new-test` is mandatory.

rules.name: use-retry-not-settimeout
rules.evidence: Use `retry()` from `next-test-utils` instead of `setTimeout` for waiting

rules.name: no-check-deprecated
rules.evidence: Do NOT use `check()` - it is deprecated. Use `retry()` + `expect()` instead

rules.name: prefer-fixture-dirs
rules.evidence: Prefer real fixture directories over inline `files` objects ... Avoid - inline file definitions are harder to maintain

rules.name: pr-description-llm-marker
rules.evidence: When writing PR descriptions, you MUST include the following HTML comment at the bottom of the description: <!-- NEXT_JS_LLM_PR -->

rules.name: no-ai-attribution-commit
rules.evidence: Do NOT add "Generated with Claude Code" or co-author footers to commits or PRs

rules.name: no-ai-attribution-pr
rules.evidence: Do NOT add "Generated with Claude Code" or co-author footers to commits or PRs

rules.name: no-pr-ready
rules.evidence: Do NOT mark PRs as "ready for review" (`gh pr ready`) - leave PRs in draft mode and let the user decide when to mark them ready

rules.name: pre-commit-lint-format
rules.evidence: Pre-validate before committing to avoid slow lint-staged failures (~2 min each): Run exactly what the pre-commit hook runs on your changed files

rules.name: flags-wiring-config
rules.evidence: New flags: add type in `config-shared.ts`, schema in `config-schema.ts`, and `define-env.ts` when used in user-bundled code.

rules.name: dce-safe-require
rules.evidence: Keep `require()` behind compile-time `if/else` branches for DCE (avoid early-return/throw patterns).

rules.name: edge-flags-force-false
rules.evidence: In edge builds, force feature flags that gate Node-only imports to `false` in `define-env.ts`.

rules.name: entry-base-boundary
rules.evidence: `react-server-dom-webpack/*` imports must stay in `entry-base.ts`; consume via component module exports elsewhere.

rules.name: app-page-no-relative-require
rules.evidence: You cannot require internal modules with relative paths because they won't be resolvable from the user's project. Instead, export new helpers from `entry-base.ts`.

rules.name: router-act-no-browser-back
rules.evidence: Never use `browser.back()` to return to a page where accordion links are already visible — BFCache restores state and triggers uncontrolled re-prefetches.

rules.name: rust-avoid-super-imports
rules.evidence: Avoid adding new `super::` imports except in inline `mod` blocks (e.g. `mod tests { ... }`) — prefer `crate::`-rooted paths.

rules.name: docs-highlight-line-check
rules.evidence: Double-check highlights by counting lines from 1 within each code block.

rules.name: flag-nonstandard-header-read
rules.evidence: When reviewing PRs: if new code reads a request header that is not a standard HTTP header ... flag it for security review.

rules.name: never-commit-env-secrets
rules.evidence: Never commit local secret files; if documenting env setup, use placeholder-only examples.

rules.name: no-ssh-workaround-remote-mutate
rules.evidence: Do not switch remotes to HTTPS, mutate remote URLs, retry repeatedly, or attempt another authentication workaround unless the user explicitly requests it.

rules.name: ssh-preflight-before-risky-git
rules.evidence: Before a force-push or stack rebase that may hydrate partial-clone objects, prefer a lightweight SSH preflight.

rules.name: capture-output-dont-rerun
rules.evidence: Never re-run the same test suite with different grep filters. Capture output once to a file, then read from it

uncompilable: Keep commit messages concise and descriptive — general style guidance, not a checkable action.
PR descriptions should focus on what changed and why — content-quality guidance, not action-bindable.
Prioritize blocking failures first: build, lint, types, then tests — triage-priority heuristic, a judgment call not a tool trigger.
Assume failures are real until disproven; use 'Known Flaky Tests' as context, not auto-dismissal — judgment heuristic.
For module-resolution/build-graph fixes, use the normal mode-specific test command — contextual judgment that can't be detected via a regex trigger.
CI failure remediation recipes (cargo fmt for rust check failures, prettier --write for lint failures) — reactive troubleshooting steps tied to an error state, not a preventable pre-call trigger.
When the user asks about CI failures/PR reviews/PR status, run the pr-status script — trigger is user-intent classification, not a tool-call pattern.
Bundler selection reference (Turbopack is default, --webpack flag, no --no-turbopack flag) — documentation catalog, no obligation attached.
Grep first then read targeted ranges; don't read generated files (dist/, node_modules/, .next/) — bound to the Read tool, outside the supported Bash|Edit|Write trigger set.
Never re-read the same file section without code changes in between — same, Read-tool-bound.
Batch edits before building, then run one build instead of build-per-edit — planning heuristic, not tied to one specific tool call.
External API calls: save response to a variable/file rather than re-fetching — workflow efficiency heuristic, not reliably one exact trigger.
Cache components enables PPR by default (test-suite behavior note) — informational, no obligation.
Quick smoke testing with toy apps via node dev server + curl — optional technique, not an obligation.
Mode-specific tests need skipStart: true + manual next.start() in beforeAll — descriptive test-writing detail, not cleanly regex-triggerable.
Don't rely on exact log messages; filter by content patterns — general test-writing wisdom, not action-bindable.
cargo fmt uses ASCII ordering — trivial informational tip.
Internal compiler error (ICE)? Delete incremental compilation artifacts and retry — reactive troubleshooting for an error state.
Node.js source map notes (findSourceMap flag requirement, path format variance, process.cwd() variance) — informational/troubleshooting notes.
Stale native binary troubleshooting after branch switch — reactive troubleshooting, not preventable via a pre-call trigger.
If a required secret is missing locally, stop and ask the user rather than inventing placeholder credentials — behavioral judgment call, not a specific detectable tool-call pattern.
When sharing command output, summarize and redact sensitive-looking values — applies to the agent's response text, not a tool call.
Validate edge bundling regressions with a specific standalone.test.ts command — narrow contextual tip tied to one bug class, not a general trigger.
Use __NEXT_SHOW_IGNORE_LISTED=true for full internal stack traces — optional debugging tip, no obligation attached.
define-env.ts affects user bundling but not pre-compiled runtime bundle internals — factual clarification, not an obligation.
